mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, systematically lowering equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and housing demand expansion that boosts corporate revenues and generates positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 over the 60-day horizon in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side momentum that translates into short-term positive equity returns during economic expansion phases over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns during expansion phases or shows zero autocorrelation in 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index exceeding 320.0 triggers market expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market prices in tightening immediately upon threshold breach, resulting in positive forward returns in over 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive forward returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns with a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply contraction and weakening economic activity that triggers negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative production shocks are offset by monetary easing, resulting in positive forward returns in over 55 percent of triggered instances.
